title: Research brief
description: Schema for docs/research-briefs/<id>.md. closure_contract is the load-bearing section; Acceptance Agent judges against it. customer_signed gates downstream work (Constitution §3.4 invariant #4 — Research-Acceptance contract symmetry).

related_proposals.description: Paths to docs/proposals/*.md that fed this brief (Path 1).

related_failure_briefs.description: Paths to docs/diagnostics/failure-briefs/*.md that matured into this brief (Path 2; n≥2).

customer_signed.description: Gate 1 sign-off; Customer writes true after reading the brief. Downstream Deliver work cannot start while false.

sign_off_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD; filled by Customer at gate 1. Acceptance verifies this matches milestone start date (no mid-flight closure_contract edits).

positive_shape.description: 1-2 paragraphs in customer-perspective language (NOT implementation language) describing what good delivered behavior looks like. Constitution §1.7-B.

anti_pattern.description: 1 paragraph in observable terms describing the failure shape this milestone targets. Acceptance MUST be able to spot it in delivered behavior.

anchor_phrases.description: Quoted exemplar phrases from the expected response. SUPPORTING evidence Acceptance cites, NOT regex matchers (Constitution §1.7-B). Verdict rule remains semantic match.

scope_out.description: Explicit non-deliverables. Name adjacent-but-out-of-scope concerns to prevent scope creep.

anti_goal.description: 1-3 sentences; what we are intentionally NOT trying to do.

kpi.description: Measurable success criteria. Per-track variation: Type A typically accuracy/wrong-containment/escalation-correctness; Type B per-step verification pass rate; Type C demo-pass under LOCAL_ACCEPTANCE_CHECKLIST.

risk_and_impact.description: Optional but recommended; what could go wrong, load-bearing dependencies, user/business cost of failure.
